input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a MAUI `CollectionView` with single selection and pull-to-refresh:

**XAML:**

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel bindings needed:**

| Property/Command | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `Items` | `ObservableCollection<Item>` | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` | `Item?` | Two-way; holds current selection |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `IAsyncRelayCommand` | Fires on selection, navigates to detail |
| `RefreshCommand` | `IAsyncRelayCommand` | Fires on pull-to-refresh, calls API |
| `IsRefreshing` | `bool` | Two-way; set `false` when reload completes |

**ViewModel sketch (CommunityToolkit.Mvvm):**

```csharp
public partial class ListViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool isRefreshing;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(await _api.GetItemsAsync());
        IsRefreshing = false;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task SelectionChangedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;
        await _nav.GoToAsync("DetailPage", new() { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });
        SelectedItem = null; // reset so user can re-select same item
    }
}
```

**Key points:**

- `RefreshView` wraps the `CollectionView`—it provides the pull gesture.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` after data loads to dismiss the spinner.
- Reset `SelectedItem` to `null` after navigation so the same row can be tapped again.
- `SelectionMode="Single"` ensures only one item highlights at a time.
